lede: A look at December 20 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Facundo Pellistri and Jonah Hill, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2024 Magdeburg car attack
events.desc: Six people are killed and over 200 are injured when an anti-Islam activist drives a car into a crowd at a Christmas market in Magdeburg, Germany.

events.title: Portrait of Suzanne Bloch
events.desc: The Portrait of Suzanne Bloch (1904), by the Spanish artist Pablo Picasso, and O Lavrador de Café by Brazilian modernist painter Cândido Portinari, are stolen from the São Paulo Museum of Art in Brazil. Both will be recovered a few weeks later.

events.title: American Airlines Flight 965
events.desc: American Airlines Flight 965, a Boeing 757, crashes into a mountain 50 km north of Cali, Colombia, killing 159 of the 163 people on board.

events.title: MV Doña Paz
events.desc: In the worst peacetime sea disaster, the passenger ferry Doña Paz sinks after colliding with the oil tanker MT Vector in the Tablas Strait of the Philippines, killing an estimated 4,000 people (1,749 official).

events.title: Murder of Jonelle Matthews
events.desc: Disappearance of Jonelle Matthews from Greeley, Colorado. Her remains were discovered on July 23, 2019, located about 24 km (15 mi) southeast of Jonelle's home. The cause of death "was a gunshot wound to the head."

events.title: Zodiac Killer
events.desc: The Zodiac Killer murders his first two officially confirmed victims, David Arthur Faraday and Betty Lou Jensen, on Lake Herman Road in Benicia, California, United States.

events.title: Experimental Breeder Reactor I
events.desc: The EBR-1 in Arco, Idaho becomes the first nuclear power plant to generate electricity. The electricity powered four light bulbs.

events.title: It's a Wonderful Life
events.desc: It's a Wonderful Life premieres at the Globe Theatre in New York to mixed reviews.

events.title: Cheka
events.desc: Cheka, the first Soviet secret police force, is founded.

events.title: Louisiana Purchase
events.desc: The Louisiana Purchase is completed at a ceremony in New Orleans.

births.name: Spud Davis
births.line: American baseball player, coach, and manager (died 1984)

deaths.text: Adrian Mitchell, English author, poet, and playwright
John Steinbeck, American novelist and short story writer, Nobel Prize laureate
João Ferreira Sardo, the founder of Gafanha da Nazaré, also known as Prior Sardo (born 1873).
Carlo Innocenzo Frugoni, Italian poet and academic

observances.text: Abolition of Slavery Day, also known as Fête des Cafres (Réunion, French Guiana)
